# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same name
# /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there. To override
# only specific settings, add a file with a lexically later
# name in /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there.
#
# For more information, see sysctl.conf(5) and sysctl.d(5).
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0

scan_output: Vulnerability sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_all_accept_source_route not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message:
**Remediation Summary**

The kernel parameter **net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route** has been disabled and made persistent on the Rocky Linux 10 host.

| Action | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Persistent configuration** | Created `/etc/sysctl.d/99-security.conf` containing `net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0` (mode 0644, owned by root). |
| **Runtime change** | Executed `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route=0`. |
| **Reload** | Ran `sysctl --system` to apply all sysctl files, including the new one. |
| **Verification** | Confirmed with `sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route` that the value is `0`. |

The OpenSCAP finding **sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_all_accept_source_route** is now resolved. No SSH or critical services were affected, and the change is fully reversible if needed.

reasoning_messages.reasoning: Check all sysctl config files (etc/sysctl.conf, /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf, /run/sysctl.d/*.conf, /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf) for any active lines setting net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route. If found, comment them out or change the value to 0. Then guarantee persistence by adding or editing /etc/sysctl.d/99-security.conf to contain the line “net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0”.
